subject: COAS Web Brief - May 26, 2026
paragraph: Mathematics continued to lead all departments with 81,510 sessions, a signal that high-quality, well-maintained content drives real audience engagement, the kind of engagement the rest of the college should be benchmarking against. Our mobile score of 59 remains 11 points below the target of 70, meaning a significant share of that traffic is landing on pages that don't perform well on the devices most visitors are actually using. I'm going to look into some solutions for this. Meanwhile, we are focused on getting a student worker in place and active as broken links climbed to 1,465 across the college's web properties this week, a volume that erodes the experience for the 512,900 new users who found us over the past 7 days and risks undermining the strong search visibility we depend on to reach prospective students and researchers. And with our accessibility and SEO scores both sitting at 83 against a peer average of 85, closing those gaps alongside the broken-link backlog will be the clearest path to measurable gains.
priorities:
? Getting server/network strategy approved by Jennifer Hutchinson, emailed Thursday, will follow up at COB Wednesday.

? Once that strategy is approved and spun up, I will work with Edward to start the CHEM migration. 

? Discussing PHIL strategy with Kailynn (and look through documentation) and communicate with Hyo Won to get project started.

? Getting student worker listing up and hired.

? Working on connecting Freshdesk and ClickUp.
note: * Meeting on Wednesday is now optional as we discussed the server plan before Pam's retirement. I'm going to leave it on the calendar in case Jennifer has alternative suggestions.
